   <div id="header">
        <h1>🐘 HEC Spatial Decision Support System - Anuradhapura</h1>
        <div style="display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 15px;">
            
            <button onclick="window.open('https://forms.gle/YOUR_GOOGLE_FORM_LINK_HERE', '_blank')" style="background-color: #e74c3c; color: white; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid #c0392b; border-radius: 20px; font-weight: bold; font-size: 13px; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 2px 5px rgba(0,0,0,0.2); display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font-family: inherit;">
                <span>🚨</span> Report New Incident
            </button>

    <div class="container">
        <div id="sidebar">
            
            <div class="chapter active" id="chap-intro" data-lat="8.35" data-lng="80.45" data-zoom="9">
                <img src="cover.jpg" alt="HEC Anuradhapura Crisis">
                
                <h2>1. The HEC Crisis in Anuradhapura</h2>
                <p>The Human-Elephant Conflict (HEC) in Sri Lanka is one of the most severe conservation and socio-economic challenges in Asia. Among all districts, <strong>Anuradhapura remains the epicenter of this crisis</strong>. Home to vast ancient irrigation networks and dense dry-zone forests, the district provides an ideal, yet volatile, intersection where human agriculture and elephant habitats collide.</p>
                <p>As human settlements expand and infrastructure development encroaches upon traditional elephant corridors, elephants are increasingly forced to navigate through agricultural lands. The loss of their natural foraging grounds has driven these highly intelligent mega-herbivores to depend on human-cultivated crops—such as paddy, maize, and banana—which offer a highly concentrated nutritional reward.</p>
                <p>This Spatial Decision Support System (SDSS) is designed to move beyond traditional record-keeping. By visualizing historical incident data, analyzing proximity to forest buffers, and calculating emergency routing, this platform empowers the Department of Wildlife Conservation (DWLC) and local communities to make data-driven, preemptive decisions.</p>
                <p style="background:#f9f9f9;padding:10px;border-radius:6px;font-size:13px;color:#555;border-left:3px solid #bdc3c7;"><i>💡 Use the <b>DS Division filter</b> above the map to explore specific incidents. Toggle the <b>Risk Heatmap</b> in the top right layer control to visualize density.</i></p>
            </div>
            
            <div class="chapter" id="chap-horowpathana" data-lat="8.53" data-lng="80.88" data-zoom="11">
                <h2>2. High Risk: Horowpathana Zone</h2>
                <p>Spatial analysis of recent incident data reveals that the <strong>Horowpathana Divisional Secretariat Division</strong> in northeastern Anuradhapura is experiencing an alarming concentration of conflict events, particularly resulting in elephant fatalities. This is not a coincidence.</p>
                <p>Horowpathana is geographically situated adjacent to major forest reserves and serves as a critical, yet highly fragmented, corridor for elephant migration. A major contributing factor is the establishment of the <em>Horowpathana Elephant Holding Ground</em>, a fenced enclosure designed to hold "problem elephants." However, the surrounding areas continue to suffer from intense raiding by resident herds.</p>
                <p>By observing the spatial distribution on the map, it becomes evident that a vast majority of incidents cluster tightly within the <strong><span style="color:#ff3333;">500-meter High-Risk Buffer Zone</span></strong>—the transitional "edge habitat" immediately bordering protected forests. Here, poorly maintained electric fences and blocked movement pathways force elephants directly into village backyards, resulting in retaliatory killings via electrocution, poisoning, and Hakka Patas (jaw exploders).</p>
                <p style="background:#f9f9f9;padding:10px;border-radius:6px;font-size:13px;color:#555;border-left:3px solid #bdc3c7;"><i>💡 Zoom into the cluster in the northeast. Turn on the <b>🌲 Forest Risk Zones</b> layer to see how closely human activities overlap with the critical 500m danger boundary.</i></p>
            </div>
            
            <div class="chapter" id="chap-mahavilachchiya" data-lat="8.20" data-lng="80.30" data-zoom="11">
                <h2>3. Human Casualties Cluster</h2>
                <p>While the northeastern zones show high elephant mortality, the spatial patterns of <strong>human fatalities reveal a distinct and tragic cluster in southwestern Anuradhapura</strong>, specifically within the <strong>Thalawa</strong> and <strong>Mahavilachchiya</strong> divisions.</p>
                <p>These areas border the expansive Wilpattu National Park. The dynamics of conflict here are different; they are heavily driven by seasonal droughts and the scarcity of water inside the park, which pushes elephants outward into human-dominated landscapes in search of sustenance.</p>
                <p>Data indicates that over 75% of fatal encounters occur between dusk and dawn. Victims are predominantly male farmers who are forced to engage in hazardous nighttime crop-guarding from makeshift tree huts (Pela). Without early warning systems and armed only with firecrackers or flashlights, these farmers face extreme danger when attempting to drive away massive, often aggressive, lone bull elephants accustomed to human presence.</p>
                <p>The clustering of human deaths in these specific divisions highlights a critical need for targeted interventions, such as the construction of robust community-based electric fences and the deployment of advanced early warning sensors in Thalawa and Mahavilachchiya.</p>
            </div>
            
            <div class="chapter" id="chap-end" data-lat="8.35" data-lng="80.45" data-zoom="9">
                <h2>4. Emergency Response Routing</h2>
                <p>In the unpredictable landscape of HEC, the speed of emergency response can mean the difference between property damage and the loss of life. Historically, rural communities have suffered from delayed interventions due to poor road networks and long distances to the nearest Department of Wildlife Conservation (DWLC) outposts.</p>
                <p>To bridge this critical operational gap, this dashboard features an integrated <strong>Emergency Response Routing Engine</strong>. By leveraging OpenStreetMap network data, the system instantly calculates the shortest drivable path from the nearest active Wildlife Ranger Station to any reported incident location.</p>
                <p>This functionality not only assists field officers in navigating to active conflict zones rapidly but also serves as a strategic planning tool for policymakers. By analyzing areas where the estimated response time exceeds 45 minutes, authorities can scientifically justify the establishment of new, localized rapid-response units or community defense teams.</p>
                <p style="background:#eaf3fb;padding:10px;border-radius:6px;font-size:13px;color:#2980b9;border-left:3px solid #2980b9; margin-top: 15px;"><b>🚨 Try it out:</b> Click on any incident marker (🔴/🟡) on the map and select <i>"Route to Nearest Ranger"</i>. The system will automatically draw the optimal path and calculate the response distance.</p>
            </div>
        </div>

        var map = L.map('map').setView([8.35, 80.45], 9);
        var satellite = L.tileLayer('https://server.arcgisonline.com/ArcGIS/rest/services/World_Imagery/MapServer/tile/{z}/{y}/{x}', { maxZoom: 18 }).addTo(map);
        var osm = L.tileLayer('https://{s}.tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png', { maxZoom: 18 });

        if (typeof L.Control.Measure !== 'undefined') {
            var measureControl = new L.Control.Measure({ position: 'topleft', primaryLengthUnit: 'kilometers', secondaryLengthUnit: 'meters', primaryAreaUnit: 'hectares', activeColor: '#d35400', completedColor: '#2c3e50' });
            measureControl.addTo(map);
        }

        var drawnItems = new L.FeatureGroup();
        map.addLayer(drawnItems);

        var drawControl = new L.Control.Draw({
            position: 'topleft',
            edit: { featureGroup: drawnItems },
            draw: { polygon: { allowIntersection: false, showArea: true }, polyline: true, rectangle: true, circle: true, marker: true, circlemarker: false }
        });
        map.addControl(drawControl);

        map.on(L.Draw.Event.CREATED, function (e) {
            var layer = e.layer;
            drawnItems.addLayer(layer);
        });

        var rangerStations = [
            { name: "Horowpathana Wildlife Office", lat: 8.52, lng: 80.88 },
            { name: "Wilpattu Border Ranger", lat: 8.40, lng: 80.05 },
            { name: "Ritigala Range Office", lat: 8.11, lng: 80.65 }
        ];
        var stationsLayer = L.layerGroup();
        rangerStations.forEach(s => {
            var icon = L.divIcon({html: '🛡️', className: 'station-icon', iconSize: [24,24]});
            L.marker([s.lat, s.lng], {icon: icon, zIndexOffset: 1000})
             .bindPopup(`<b style="color:#27ae60;">${s.name}</b><br>Active Patrol Unit`)
             .addTo(stationsLayer);
        });

        var markers = L.markerClusterGroup();
        var rawPointsGroup = L.layerGroup(); 
        var heatLayer = L.heatLayer([], { radius: 25, blur: 18, maxZoom: 10, gradient: {0.4: 'yellow', 0.65: 'orange', 1: 'red'} }).addTo(map);
        var routingControl = null;

        var actualPointsData = (Array.isArray(geojsonData) && geojsonData.length === 1) ? geojsonData[0] : geojsonData;
        var actualBufferData = (Array.isArray(bufferData) && bufferData.length === 1) ? bufferData[0] : bufferData;

        var features = actualPointsData.features || [];
        var dsSet = new Set();
        features.forEach(f => {
            var ds = f.properties.DS_Division || f.properties.DS_Divisio;
            if(ds) dsSet.add(ds);
        });
        var dsSelect = document.getElementById('ds-filter');
        Array.from(dsSet).sort().forEach(ds => {
            var opt = document.createElement('option');
            opt.value = ds;
            opt.innerHTML = ds;
            dsSelect.appendChild(opt);
        });

        window.findNearestRanger = function(lat, lng) {
            var incidentLoc = L.latLng(lat, lng);
            var nearest = rangerStations[0];
            var minDist = incidentLoc.distanceTo(L.latLng(nearest.lat, nearest.lng));

            for(var i=1; i<rangerStations.length; i++) {
                var dist = incidentLoc.distanceTo(L.latLng(rangerStations[i].lat, rangerStations[i].lng));
                if(dist < minDist) { minDist = dist; nearest = rangerStations[i]; }
            }

            if(routingControl) { map.removeControl(routingControl); }

            routingControl = L.Routing.control({
                waypoints: [ L.latLng(nearest.lat, nearest.lng), incidentLoc ],
                routeWhileDragging: false,
                addWaypoints: false,
                showAlternatives: false,
                fitSelectedRoutes: true,
                lineOptions: { styles: [{color: '#2980b9', opacity: 0.8, weight: 6}] }
            }).addTo(map);
        };

        function renderMapData(selectedDS) {
            markers.clearLayers();
            rawPointsGroup.clearLayers(); 
            var heatData = [];
            var totalIncidents = 0, elephantDeaths = 0, humanDeaths = 0;

            function createPopup(feature, latlng, color, icon) {
                var ds = feature.properties.DS_Division || feature.properties.DS_Divisio || "Unknown";
                var type = feature.properties.Incident_Type || feature.properties.Incident_T || "Unknown";
                var name = feature.properties.Victim_Name || "N/A";
                return `<div style="font-family: 'Segoe UI', Tahoma, sans-serif;">
                        <h4 style="margin: 0 0 5px 0; color: ${color}; border-bottom: 1px solid #ccc; padding-bottom: 3px;">${icon} ${type}</h4>
                        <b>DS Division:</b> ${ds}<br><b>Victim:</b> ${name}<br>
                        <button class="route-btn" onclick="findNearestRanger(${latlng.lat}, ${latlng.lng})">🚨 Route to Nearest Ranger</button>
                        </div>`;
            }

            var pointConfig = {
                filter: function(f) { return selectedDS === "ALL" || (f.properties.DS_Division || f.properties.DS_Divisio) === selectedDS; },
                pointToLayer: function (f, latlng) {
                    var type = f.properties.Incident_Type || f.properties.Incident_T || "Unknown";
                    var markerColor = type.includes("Human") ? "#e74c3c" : "#f1c40f"; 
                    var markerIcon = type.includes("Human") ? "🚶" : "🐘";
                    
                    heatData.push([latlng.lat, latlng.lng, 0.6]); 
                    totalIncidents++;
                    if (type.includes("Elephant")) elephantDeaths++; else if (type.includes("Human")) humanDeaths++;

                    return L.circleMarker(latlng, { radius: 7, fillColor: markerColor, color: "#333", weight: 1, fillOpacity: 0.9 })
                            .bindPopup(createPopup(f, latlng, markerColor, markerIcon));
                }
            };

            var clusterLayer = L.geoJSON(actualPointsData, pointConfig);
            totalIncidents = 0; elephantDeaths = 0; humanDeaths = 0; heatData = [];
            var rawLayer = L.geoJSON(actualPointsData, pointConfig);

            markers.addLayer(clusterLayer);
            rawPointsGroup.addLayer(rawLayer); 
            heatLayer.setLatLngs(heatData); 

            document.getElementById('kpi-total').innerText = totalIncidents;
            document.getElementById('kpi-elephant').innerText = elephantDeaths;
            document.getElementById('kpi-human').innerText = humanDeaths;

            if (selectedDS !== "ALL" && markers.getLayers().length > 0) {
                map.flyToBounds(markers.getBounds(), { padding: [50, 50], duration: 1.5 });
            } else if (selectedDS === "ALL") {
                map.flyTo([8.35, 80.45], 9, { duration: 1.5 });
            }
        }

        dsSelect.addEventListener('change', function(e) { renderMapData(e.target.value); });
        
        map.addLayer(markers);
        stationsLayer.addTo(map);
        renderMapData("ALL");

        var riskZones = L.geoJSON(actualBufferData, {
            style: function(f) {
                var d = f.properties.distance || f.properties.DISTANCE; 
                return d === 500 ? { color: "#ff3333", dashArray: "5, 5", weight: 2, fillColor: "#ff3333", fillOpacity: 0.15 } 
                                 : { color: "#ff9933", dashArray: "5, 5", weight: 2, fillColor: "#ff9933", fillOpacity: 0.1 }; 
            }
        });

        var baseMaps = { "Satellite View": satellite, "Street Map": osm };
        var overlayMaps = { 
            "Incident Clusters": markers,
            "Raw Points (Individual)": rawPointsGroup,
            "Risk Heatmap": heatLayer,
            "🌲 Forest Risk Zones": riskZones,
            "🛡️ Wildlife Ranger Stations": stationsLayer,
            "✏️ Drawn Items": drawnItems
        };
        L.control.layers(baseMaps, overlayMaps, {collapsed: false}).addTo(map);

        var chapters = document.querySelectorAll('.chapter');
        var observerOptions = { root: document.getElementById('sidebar'), rootMargin: '0px', threshold: 0.5 };
        var observer = new IntersectionObserver(function(entries) {
            entries.forEach(entry => {
                if (entry.isIntersecting) {
                    chapters.forEach(c => c.classList.remove('active'));
                    entry.target.classList.add('active');
                    if(document.getElementById('ds-filter').value === "ALL") {
                        var lat = parseFloat(entry.target.getAttribute('data-lat'));
                        var lng = parseFloat(entry.target.getAttribute('data-lng'));
                        var zoom = parseInt(entry.target.getAttribute('data-zoom'));
                        map.flyTo([lat, lng], zoom, { animate: true, duration: 1.5 });
                    }
                }
            });
        }, observerOptions);
        chapters.forEach(chapter => { observer.observe(chapter); });

    </script>
</body>
</html>
